item.title: Therefore, Moggallāna, you should train thus: 'I will not speak provoking words.' Thus, Moggallāna, you should train. When there are provoking words, Moggallāna, then much…
item.description:
Therefore, Moggallāna, you should train thus: 'I will not speak provoking words.' Thus, Moggallāna, you should train. When there are provoking words, Moggallāna, then much back-and-forth speech is to be expected. When there is much back-and-forth speech, then there is agitation. For one who is agitated, there is no sense restraint. For one without sense restraint, the mind is far from concentration. Moggallāna, I do not praise all kinds of association, but neither do I refrain from praising all kinds of association. Truly, Moggallāna, I do not praise association with laypeople for those gone forth. But association with dwellings that are quiet, free from noise, free from the breath of crowds, solitary, remote from people, suitable for meditative seclusion — I praise association with such dwellings.

From: Aṅguttara Nikāya 7.61
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Bhikkhus, as long as the bhikkhus do not have evil friends, evil companions, evil associates, then the bhikkhus will prosper and not decline.
item.description:
Bhikkhus, as long as the bhikkhus do not have evil friends, evil companions, evil associates, then the bhikkhus will prosper and not decline.

From: Dīgha Nikāya 16 – Mahāparinibbānasutta
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: The wise, abandoning dark states, Develop bright states, Leaving home for homelessness, In seclusion, hard to delight in. Seek delight in Nibbāna, Abandon sensual pleasures,…
item.description:
The wise, abandoning dark states,
Develop bright states,
Leaving home for homelessness,
In seclusion, hard to delight in.

Seek delight in Nibbāna,
Abandon sensual pleasures, have nothing,
The wise cleanse themselves
Of defilements from within.

Those with right mind,
Well develop the factors of enlightenment,
Abandon all attachments,
Rejoicing, not clinging,
Without taints, radiant,
They live at peace in the world.

From: Dhammapada 76-89
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Elder Bhūta: When the wise person sees that old age and death are suffering, The ordinary person, not seeing, grasps suffering. Having fully understood suffering,…
item.description:
Elder Bhūta:

When the wise person sees that old age and death are suffering,
The ordinary person, not seeing, grasps suffering.
Having fully understood suffering, mindful, practicing jhāna,
He finds no happiness superior to this happiness.

When craving is destroyed — craving that brings suffering and poison,
Creates proliferation and bondage, pushes toward suffering —
Having destroyed craving, mindful, practicing jhāna,
He finds no happiness superior to this happiness.

When with wisdom one sees the supreme good path,
Consisting of eight parts in two groups, purifying all defilements,
Having seen with wisdom, mindful, practicing jhāna,
He finds no happiness superior to this happiness.

When one develops the path — sorrowless, stainless,
Unconditioned, peaceful path, purifying all defilements,
Cutting the bonds of fetters, mindful, practicing jhāna,
He finds no happiness superior to this happiness.

When thundercloud drums roar in the sky,
Along the bird's path, a dense rain pours down,
A bhikkhu going to a cave, developing, sits in meditation,
He finds no happiness superior to this happiness.

When on the riverbanks, the forest garlands bloom and shine
With many colors and hues,
With a relaxed mind, sitting in meditation on the riverbank,
He finds no happiness superior to this happiness.

When in the dead of night, the forest silent,
A torrential rain pours down,
Tusked beasts are roaring as they live,
A bhikkhu going to a mountainside sits silently in meditation,
He finds no happiness superior to this happiness.

When applied and sustained thought are subdued,
In a mountain cave, sitting in meditation at ease,
Without fear, without obstruction,
He finds no happiness superior to this happiness.

When sitting with joy and bliss, without defilement, without sorrow,
Unconfined, free from craving, not struck by the dart,
All taints ended, that one sits in meditation,
He finds no happiness superior to this happiness.

From: Theragāthā 9.1
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Possessing these three qualities, bhikkhus, a woman, after death, with the breakup of the body, is mostly reborn in a plane of misery, a bad…
item.description:
Possessing these three qualities, bhikkhus, a woman, after death, with the breakup of the body, is mostly reborn in a plane of misery, a bad destination, a lower realm, hell. What three? Here, bhikkhus, a woman, in the morning, lives at home with a mind obsessed by stinginess and miserliness; at noon, lives at home with a mind obsessed by envy and jealousy; in the evening, lives at home with a mind obsessed by sensual desire. Possessing these three qualities, bhikkhus, a woman, after death, with the breakup of the body, is mostly reborn in a plane of misery, a bad destination, a lower realm, hell.

From: Saṃyutta Nikāya 37.4
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: - There are these four types of meditators, bhikkhus. What are the four? Here, bhikkhus, a certain meditator is skilled in attainment in concentration but…
item.description:
- There are these four types of meditators, bhikkhus. What are the four? Here, bhikkhus, a certain meditator is skilled in attainment in concentration but not skilled in duration in concentration. Here, bhikkhus, a certain meditator is skilled in duration in concentration but not skilled in attainment in concentration. Here, bhikkhus, a certain meditator is skilled neither in attainment nor in duration in concentration. Here, bhikkhus, a certain meditator is skilled both in attainment and in duration in concentration. Among these four meditators, the one who is skilled both in attainment and in duration in concentration is the foremost, best, chief, highest, and most excellent.

From: Saṃyutta Nikāya 34.11
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: If a bhikkhu teaches the Dhamma for the purpose of disenchantment, dispassion, and the cessation of ignorance, that is sufficient for him to be called…
item.description:
If a bhikkhu teaches the Dhamma for the purpose of disenchantment, dispassion, and the cessation of ignorance, that is sufficient for him to be called a teacher of Dhamma.
If a bhikkhu practices for the purpose of disenchantment, dispassion, and the cessation of ignorance, that is sufficient for him to be called a bhikkhu who practices the Dhamma in accordance with the Dhamma.
If a bhikkhu is liberated by non-clinging through disenchantment, dispassion, and the cessation of ignorance, that is sufficient for him to be called a bhikkhu who has attained Nibbāna in this very life.

From: Saṃyutta Nikāya 12.16
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Bhikkhus, what is Right Effort? Bhikkhus, here a bhikkhu generates desire for the non-arising of unarisen evil, unwholesome states; he makes an effort, arouses energy,…
item.description:
Bhikkhus, what is Right Effort? Bhikkhus, here a bhikkhu generates desire for the non-arising of unarisen evil, unwholesome states; he makes an effort, arouses energy, applies his mind, and strives. He generates desire for the abandoning of arisen evil, unwholesome states; he makes an effort, arouses energy, applies his mind, and strives. He generates desire for the arising of unarisen wholesome states; he makes an effort, arouses energy, applies his mind, and strives. He generates desire for the maintenance, non-decline, increase, expansion, and fulfillment of arisen wholesome states; he makes an effort, arouses energy, applies his mind, and strives. Bhikkhus, this is called Right Effort.

From: Dīgha Nikāya 22 – Mahāsatipaṭṭhānasutta
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Hemāvata: By the arising of what does the world arise? By the associating with what is the world established? By clinging to what is the…
item.description:
Hemāvata:
By the arising of what does the world arise?
By the associating with what is the world established?
By clinging to what is the world troubled?
By the arising of what is the world tormented?

The Blessed One:
By the arising of six the world arises,
By associating with six it is established,
By clinging to six the world is troubled,
By the arising of six the world is tormented.

From: Sutta Nipāta 1.9
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: And, Sāriputta, what is the supreme respect that a bhikkhu with taints destroyed shows to the Tathāgata or to the Tathāgata's teaching?" - "Here, Venerable…
item.description:
And, Sāriputta, what is the supreme respect that a bhikkhu with taints destroyed shows to the Tathāgata or to the Tathāgata's teaching?" - "Here, Venerable Sir, a bhikkhu with taints destroyed lives respecting and revering the Teacher; lives respecting and revering the Dhamma; lives respecting and revering the Saṅgha; lives respecting and revering the training; lives respecting and revering concentration. This, Venerable Sir, is the supreme respect that a bhikkhu with taints destroyed shows to the Tathāgata or to the Tathāgata's teaching." - "Good, good, Sāriputta! This is the supreme respect that a bhikkhu with taints destroyed shows to the Tathāgata or to the Tathāgata's teaching.

From: Saṃyutta Nikāya 48.58
📖 Read this sutta
